questions.question: What is the perimeter of a polygon?
questions.correct_answer: The sum of the lengths of its all sides.

questions.question: How to find the perimeter of a rectangle?
questions.correct_answer: Twice the sum of its length and width.

questions.question: What is the perimeter of a square?
questions.correct_answer: Four times the length of any one of its sides.

questions.question: How to estimate the area of a region?
questions.correct_answer: By breaking up such regions into unit squares, or into more general-shaped rectangles and triangles whose areas can be calculated.

questions.question: What is the formula for the perimeter of a rectangle?
How to find the area of a square?
questions.correct_answer: Length of any one of its sides multiplied by itself.

questions.question: What is the formula for the perimeter of a square?
How to find the area of a rectangle?
What is the difference between perimeter and area?
questions.correct_answer: Perimeter is the distance around a shape, while area is the amount of space inside it.

questions.question: Can two closed figures have the same area with different perimeters?

important_notes.title: Perimeter of a Polygon
important_notes.content: The perimeter is the sum of the lengths of its all sides.

important_notes.title: Area of a Square
important_notes.content: Length of any one of its sides multiplied by itself.

important_notes.title: Perimeter of a Square
important_notes.content: Four times the length of any one of its sides.

important_notes.title: Estimating Area of Region
important_notes.content: By breaking up such regions into unit squares, or into more general-shaped rectangles and triangles whose areas can be calculated.

important_notes.title: Difference Between Perimeter and Area
important_notes.content: Perimeter is the distance around a shape, while area is the amount of space inside it.

facts.fact: The word 'perimeter' comes from the Greek words 'peri' meaning around and 'meter' meaning measure.
The formula for the perimeter of a rectangle is 2(length + width).
A square has four equal sides, making it a special type of rectangle.
The area of a region can be estimated by breaking it up into smaller shapes like rectangles and triangles.
Perimeter is often used to describe the distance around a shape, while area is used to describe the amount of space inside it.
There are many real-life applications for perimeter and area, such as architecture, engineering, and design.
The concept of perimeter and area dates back thousands of years, with ancient civilizations like the Egyptians and Greeks using them in their mathematics.
Perimeter and area are used in many different fields, including science, technology, engineering, and math (STEM).
The formula for the perimeter of a square is 4(side length).
A rectangle's perimeter can be found by adding up all four sides.
